assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response explains increased preemption risk during upgrades and mentions spot instance volatility during disruption

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response mentions that spot workloads should be designed with appropriate tolerations and fault-tolerance considerations

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response actually recommends conservative/lower maxSurge settings (--max-surge 1) for spot instances, opposite of the expectation

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response does not mention or configure maxUnavailable settings at all

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response mentions planning for longer upgrade windows and considering low-demand periods when spot availability is higher

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes a specific PodDisruptionBudget YAML example and emphasizes ensuring proper PDBs

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response provides Azure CLI (az) commands, not gcloud commands which are for Google Cloud Platform

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response suggests the opposite - temporarily increasing on-demand capacity first, then upgrading spot pools, and describes gradual migration as the safest approach
